title: Pad and Spiral Bandage
content_markdown:
#### Indications
- Pad and bandage: to cover wound or lesion and slow haemorrhage.
- Spiral bandage: limb injuries, soft issues injuries, securing splints.

#### Procedure

Figure 1

Figure 2

Figure 3

- Check area for any easily removed foreign objects.

- Place combine dressing over the wound, consider placing a Non-Adhesive Dressing (NAD) over wound first (see **Figure 1**).

- Apply bandage of appropriate width: 
- Commence with two turns to anchor the bandage (see **Figure 2**).
- Work up the limb covering two thirds of the previous turn.
- Ensure adequate coverage.
- Finish with two turns and secure (see **Figure 3**).

- Check patient for comfort and frequently reassess perfusion of the distal limb and adjust as required.

- Check for continued haemorrhage and reinforce existing bandage if required.

#### Considerations
- The use of an arterial tourniquet if there is uncontrolled, life threatening haemorrhage of a limb.
